outcome.headline: Agent sank entire 10-minute budget into OCR/pixel-analysis attempts on figure.jpg and timed out without ever writing /app/answer.txt; verifier returned reward 0.

outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent OCR/pixel-analysis loop on a figure the harness cannot render
failure_modes.description: Across steps 4-24 the agent kept switching tactics (PIL inspect → install tesseract → full-image OCR → panel-slice OCR → saturation thresholding → dark-region detection → legend OCR → rotated-label OCR) without ever obtaining bar heights or x-axis labels, then ran out of budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels. The labels appear to be very small or angled text that tesseract can't read well. Let me try a completely different approach.

failure_modes.name: Required deliverable never produced before timeout
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly required writing the answer to /app/answer.txt wrapped in [ANSWER][/ANSWER]; the agent recognized this from instruction.md but never produced the file because every step was consumed by image-analysis attempts, so the verifier hit the 'ERROR: answer.txt missing' branch.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I can't read the image directly. Let me check if there's a letter mapping file that might help, and also look at the setup.py to understand the context better.

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent visual analysis loop
failure_modes.description: The agent kept changing OCR and pixel-analysis tactics for the image without converging on the answer or preserving time for output. It reached a partial interpretation of bar groups but remained stuck on label and condition mapping until timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Now I need to figure out:
1. Which group corresponds to which anatomical region
2. Which bar in each group is "Shocked"
3. Which Shocked bar has the greatest height (greatest change)

counterfactual.X: A robust recovery required a strategy pivot: timebox the visual analysis, inspect the figure with a method that exposes the image or crops directly, then commit to the mapped answer.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have produced a valid [ANSWER]C[/ANSWER] file instead of timing out.

failure_modes.name: No deliverable produced before timeout (missing answer.txt)
failure_modes.description: The instruction explicitly required writing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] to answer.txt. The agent never created /app/answer.txt at any point — not even a best-guess fallback among the five MCQ letters — so when the 10-minute harness timeout fired, the verifier had no file to parse and reward was 0. A one-line fallback write of any letter would have given a 1/5 chance instead of guaranteed zero.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: - Write your answer to `answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: early in the run (e.g. after step 4 when the image proved unreadable), the agent writes a placeholder '[ANSWER]C[/ANSWER]' to /app/answer.txt and refines it later if analysis succeeds
counterfactual.Y: answer.txt would exist at verifier time; with random guessing among 5 options the trial has ~20% pass probability instead of guaranteed 0% (and 'C' happens to be the correct letter for this seed)

failure_modes.name: Failed to fall back to 'Insufficient information' option
failure_modes.description: By step 3 the agent had confirmed it could not view the image directly; the choices list explicitly contains 'Insufficient information to answer the question'. The correct meta-move was to write [ANSWER]<letter-for-Insufficient information>[/ANSWER] and exit, accepting an incorrect-but-submitted answer rather than a no-submission timeout. The agent misidentified the problem as 'extract data from image' instead of 'select best available option under uncertainty'.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I can't view the image directly. Let me try to use Python or another tool to analyze the image.

counterfactual.X: After step 3 confirmed no vision capability, the agent should have read /app/.letter_mapping.json, located the letter mapped to 'Insufficient information to answer the question', and written it to /app/answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: That would have produced a parseable answer (still scored 0 against the ideal 'Right', but eliminates the timeout failure mode and demonstrates correct termination behavior).

failure_modes.name: Misfocused panel interpretation
failure_modes.description: The agent shifted from the requested panel c brain image to the bottom percent-change bar chart and then treated unreadable x-axis labels as the central obstacle, which sent later work into chart OCR rather than answering from panel c.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Let me focus on the bottom-left area (grid [1,0]) which seems to have a chart with "Percent change post-shock" and labels at the bottom that look like they could be anatomical regions.

failure_modes.name: Unsupported image read
failure_modes.description: At step 2, read_file on figure.jpg failed because the model cannot ingest images, forcing a fragile OCR workaround for a vision-required MCQ.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: This model does not support image input.

counterfactual.X: Immediately pivot to a vision-capable figure-reading path (or another supported image tool) right after read_file fails.
counterfactual.Y: Installed Pillow/tesseract and spent steps 4–24 on OCR/pixel heuristics without ever writing answer.txt.

failure_modes.name: Target panel misframed as chart-label extraction
failure_modes.description: After locating text suggesting panel c in the upper grid, the agent redirected effort to the lower bar chart and its x-axis labels, treating the task as decoding chart groups rather than visually identifying the greatest fluorescence change in the shock brain section.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Grid [0,1] has "LFM Imaging", "Post-challenge", and a "c" label - this might be panel c
- Grid [0,2] has "1.0" - likely part of a scale
- Grid [1,0] has a chart with values "40, 30, 20, 0, -10" and labels at the bottom
- Grid [1,1] has "Shocked", "Ketamine" labels
- Grid [1,2] has "-1.0"

The question asks about panel c and which section of the ventral habenula of the "shock" brain exhibited the greatest change in fluorescence.

Let me focus on the bottom-left area

counterfactual.X: At step 14, inspect the panel-c shock-brain image itself, identify the right section as having the greatest fluorescence change, and write [ANSWER]C[/ANSWER] to answer.txt.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would parse answer.txt and map C to the ideal text Right.

counterfactual.Y: Verifier would at least parse a letter and have a 1/5 chance of matching 'Right'; in this trial the canonical mapping was unknown to the agent so this would not guarantee success, but it eliminates the deterministic 0 from a missing file.

outcome.headline: Agent timed out in repeated OCR/pixel-analysis attempts and never wrote answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh checks that /app/answer.txt exists, extracts a letter inside [ANSWER]...[/ANSWER], maps that letter through /app/.letter_mapping.json, and awards reward 1 only if the chosen text equals the ideal answer in /tests/choices.json, which is "Right ".
outcome.what_agent_produced: The agent installed Pillow, pytesseract, tesseract-ocr, and numpy, ran repeated OCR and pixel analyses of figure.jpg, identified possible bar groups, but produced no final answer file.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent visual analysis loop
failure_modes.description: The agent kept changing OCR and pixel-analysis tactics for the image without converging on the answer or preserving time for output. It reached a partial interpretation of bar groups but remained stuck on label and condition mapping until timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Now I need to figure out:
1. Which group corresponds to which anatomical region
2. Which bar in each group is "Shocked"
3. Which Shocked bar has the greatest height (greatest change)

counterfactual.X: A robust recovery required a strategy pivot: timebox the visual analysis, inspect the figure with a method that exposes the image or crops directly, then commit to the mapped answer.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have produced a valid [ANSWER]C[/ANSWER] file instead of timing out.

failure_modes.name: Required answer artifact never written
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly required writing answer.txt, and the verifier's first hard gate is the existence and parseability of that file. The agent never executed a file write before the timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
Instruction:
- Please answer by responding with the letter of the correct answer.
- Wrap your answer with the following tags: [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] where X is the letter.
- Write your answer to `answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: At the end of step 25, write /app/answer.txt containing [ANSWER]C[/ANSWER].
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would map C to "Right " and award reward 1.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent read the allowed runtime mapping file, which lacked the correct answer, and did not access /tests/choices.json or other hidden verifier data during the run.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The verifier checks a simple MCQ answer file against a hidden ideal answer text. The visible mapping file contains only letter-to-option mapping and does not reveal correctness.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s timeout after a long OCR/pixel-analysis loop and never wrote /app/answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh requires /app/answer.txt containing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER], then checks the chosen letter maps to the ideal text in /tests/choices.json via /app/.letter_mapping.json (lines 53-64, 71-117).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer.txt; only shell/Python OCR and bar-chart pixel heuristics on figure.jpg after read_file rejected the image.

failure_modes.name: Unsupported image read
failure_modes.description: At step 2, read_file on figure.jpg failed because the model cannot ingest images, forcing a fragile OCR workaround for a vision-required MCQ.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: This model does not support image input.

counterfactual.X: Immediately pivot to a vision-capable figure-reading path (or another supported image tool) right after read_file fails.
counterfactual.Y: Installed Pillow/tesseract and spent steps 4–24 on OCR/pixel heuristics without ever writing answer.txt.

failure_modes.name: OCR loop no deliverable
failure_modes.description: From step 4 through step 24 the agent repeatedly retried OCR, cropping, and bar-pattern analysis but never produced the required [ANSWER] letter in answer.txt before AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent read /app/.letter_mapping.json (allowed; no correct_letter field) but never submitted an answer derived from that file alone.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compares agent letter to ideal text using /tests/choices.json only at verify time; setup.py removes choices.json from /app before the agent runs.

outcome.headline: The agent exhausted the 600s budget on OCR/pixel-analysis of figure.jpg and never wrote the required /app/answer.txt before timing out.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh requires /app/answer.txt with [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER], then maps the letter via /app/.letter_mapping.json against tests/choices.json ideal text (lines 53-64, 71-117).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer.txt; installed Pillow/numpy/pytesseract/tesseract and ran repeated shell OCR/bar-height heuristics on figure.jpg without selecting or submitting a letter.

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer file
failure_modes.description: instruction.md explicitly required writing a letter answer to answer.txt with [ANSWER] tags; across 24 agent steps the agent only used read_file and run_shell_command and never created the deliverable before AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write your answer to `answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: Write /app/answer.txt containing a chosen letter wrapped in [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] per the task format.
counterfactual.Y: Continued installing libraries and running OCR/pixel-analysis shell scripts without ever writing answer.txt.

failure_modes.name: OCR pipeline non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After read_file rejected figure.jpg as unsupported image input in step 2, the agent spent steps 4-24 cycling through pip installs, tesseract OCR, grid crops, bar-height pixel heuristics, and legend parsing without reaching a confident letter answer or submitting one.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: [Unsupported image file: "figure.jpg". This model does not support image input.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent read /app/.letter_mapping.json and setup.py; mapping contains option text only, not the correct letter, so this was exploration rather than answer leakage.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compares agent letter to shuffled mapping against hidden ideal in /tests/choices.json; correct letter is not exposed in /app during the run.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after 25 steps of OCR/bar-detection on figure.jpg without ever writing the required /app/answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh requires /app/answer.txt (lines 53-64), parses [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] (lines 71-79), and checks the chosen letter maps to tests/choices.json ideal "Right " via /app/.letter_mapping.json (lines 91-117).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer.txt; installed Pillow, numpy, and tesseract, then ran repeated Python scripts attempting OCR and pixel bar-height analysis on /app/figure.jpg without submitting a letter answer.

failure_modes.name: OCR analysis non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After learning at step 2 that figure.jpg could not be read as an image, the agent spent steps 4-24 installing vision dependencies and cycling through OCR, saturation, and bar-detection heuristics without identifying the shocked-region answer or converging on a letter choice.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels.

failure_modes.name: Required answer never written
failure_modes.description: The instruction read at step 1 explicitly required writing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] to answer.txt, but the agent never created that file before the run ended at step 24 mid-analysis.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write your answer to `answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: Write /app/answer.txt containing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] per the instruction once image analysis stalled or before the time limit.
counterfactual.Y: Continued only OCR/Python figure analysis through step 24 and never created answer.txt.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The agent can read /app/.letter_mapping.json but it omits the correct letter; matching the ideal in /tests/choices.json still requires answering from the figure.

outcome.headline: The agent hit the 600s execution timeout while still iterating on OCR/bar-chart analysis and never wrote the required /app/answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh requires /app/answer.txt with [ANSWER]letter[/ANSWER] (lines 71-84) and checks the letter maps to the ideal choice text via /app/.letter_mapping.json and /tests/choices.json (lines 91-117).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No /app/answer.txt; only shell-based installs and repeated Pillow/pytesseract/numpy scripts analyzing /app/figure.jpg.

failure_modes.name: OCR analysis non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After read_file could not load figure.jpg, the agent spent steps 7–24 installing OCR tooling and repeatedly refining bar/pixel heuristics without identifying the shocked condition’s maximum region or selecting a letter.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels.

failure_modes.name: Required answer file missing
failure_modes.description: The instruction required writing a tagged letter to answer.txt, but the trajectory contains no command creating /app/answer.txt before harbor raised AgentTimeoutError after 600.0 seconds.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write your answer to `answer.txt`.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent read /app/.letter_mapping.json for context but never wrote an answer letter or accessed /tests/choices.json.

issues: Task requires reading figure.jpg, but the qwen3.6-max-preview agent cannot ingest images via read_file, forcing a slow OCR workaround within the same 600s agent budget.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Scoring maps the submitted letter to ideal text using /tests/choices.json, which is not available to the agent during the run.

outcome.headline: The agent timed out after spending the full run on brittle OCR/bar-height analysis and never wrote the required answer.txt.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: test.sh requires /app/answer.txt with [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER], then checks the chosen letter maps to ideal text in /tests/choices.json via /app/.letter_mapping.json (test.sh:53-64 missing file, 71-117 parse and compare).
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer.txt; only shell/Python OCR and numpy pixel/bar analyses of /app/figure.jpg after read_file rejected the image.

failure_modes.name: OCR loop non-convergence
failure_modes.description: After step 2 reported the model cannot read images, the agent ran steps 3–24 installing Pillow/tesseract and iterating OCR/bar-height heuristics without converging on panel-c bar labels or a letter choice.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels.

failure_modes.name: Never wrote answer.txt
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly required writing a tagged letter to answer.txt, but the trajectory contains no write to answer.txt before the 600s AgentTimeoutError.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write your answer to `answer.txt`.

counterfactual.X: Write `[ANSWER]X[/ANSWER]` to `/app/answer.txt` (even a best-effort or insufficient-information letter) before timeout.
counterfactual.Y: Continued image OCR/pixel analysis for the remainder of the run without creating answer.txt.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compares agent letter to shuffled mapping against hidden ideal in /tests/choices.json; correctness requires matching figure content, not guessable from mapping alone.

aggregated_from.note: Failure modes: union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Outcome/verdicts: majority vote (step_where_lost = median). Individual passes are kept under `passes`. Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
